title: Chat say/me messages not validated for empty content
description: The receiveChatSay and receiveChatMe methods (lines 412-427) extract the message string but do not validate that it is non-empty or non-whitespace-only. A client can flood the chat with empty or whitespace-only messages that get broadcast to all connected users. Contrast with receiveMessageSendMessage (ServerController.swift line 889-892) which correctly validates !body.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines).isEmpty. The chat handlers lack this validation.
recommendation: Add empty-string validation after extracting the chat text: guard !say.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines).isEmpty else { return }. Apply the same check in receiveChatMe.
